description: Rolling-window NCL spending framework with post-AI cost evaluation

spending_model.rationale: Budget capacity is defined by trailing spending over a sliding 73-epoch window. No annual reset, no FCFS. Old withdrawals age out, replenishing capacity continuously.

variables.NCL_annual: Net Change Limit set by governance for the current period
variables.withdrawals: Sum of all approved TreasuryWithdrawals in the epoch range

variables.underspend_epochs: Count of consecutive epochs where spending < 10% of per-epoch allocation

effective_available.rationale: Prevents war-chest accumulation. Spending authorization decays, not the treasury balance itself.

minimum_threshold.description: Minimum DRep approval percentage to be eligible for funding
minimum_threshold.note: Exact value is a governance parameter. Doctrine requires a non-zero threshold exists.

tie_breaking.rationale: Minimal FCFS as last-resort tiebreaker only

treasury_withdrawals.condition: 5+ withdrawals already approved in current epoch
treasury_withdrawals.rationale: Spending cluster — marginal withdrawals get more scrutiny
Low-capacity regime — only highest-value proposals should proceed

treasury_withdrawals.condition: proposal text frames urgency based on calendar position or budget period boundary
treasury_withdrawals.rationale: Rolling window has no boundary. Calendar urgency is a manipulation signal.

treasury_withdrawals.condition: proposal budgets labor-hours for AI-assistable work without acknowledging AI workflows
treasury_withdrawals.rationale: Cost likely inflated 5-20x vs post-AI rates

treasury_withdrawals.condition: proposal cites cost comparisons from before widespread LLM availability
proposal budgets by person-months or team size rather than deliverables
lump-sum request with no milestone-based disbursement

treasury_withdrawals.rationale: Requested amount significantly exceeds estimated post-AI cost
Requested amount is extreme outlier vs post-AI cost

treasury_withdrawals_bonuses.condition: proposal priced by deliverables, not inputs
proposal explicitly accounts for AI-assisted cost reduction
proposal is small, scoped, demonstrates value before requesting follow-on

parameter_change_ncl.condition: proposed NCL increase without demonstrated demand (historical utilization < 60%)
parameter_change_ncl.rationale: Enabling spending surge without evidence of need

parameter_change_ncl.condition: proposed NCL decrease when historical utilization consistently < 40%
parameter_change_ncl.rationale: Right-sizing the budget to actual value delivery

parameter_change_ncl.condition: parameter change would remove or weaken rolling-window mechanism
parameter_change_ncl.rationale: Reversion to FCFS or annual-reset is a structural failure

info_action_budget.condition: sentiment signal aligns with rolling-window, merit-based spending
sentiment signal advocates for FCFS, annual-reset, or removal of quality gates
frames budget urgency from calendar or fiscal-year position
info_action_budget.rationale: Manipulation signal — rolling window has no boundary

rejected_models.reason: Creates land rush at epoch 0, rewards speed over quality, starves late-cycle proposals, breaks catastrophically under AI-driven proposal volume increase.
rejected_models.vote_position: NO on any governance action that implements or implies this model

rejected_models.reason: Too rigid for legitimate large proposals. Creates artificial per-epoch ceilings that fragment funding unnecessarily.
rejected_models.vote_position: NO unless rollover mechanism is included

rejected_models.reason: No constraint on treasury outflow. Invites extractive behavior.

principles: Never FCFS. Submission speed is not a merit signal.
Rolling window, not annual reset. Budget capacity is defined by recent spending, not calendar boundaries.
Quality gates are non-negotiable. DRep approval thresholds prevent low-quality flooding.
AI has changed the cost basis. Evaluate proposals against post-AI marginal costs.
Decay over accumulation. Unspent capacity compresses, preventing war-chest dynamics.
Concentration limits. No single proposal should consume a disproportionate share of remaining capacity.
Output-priced, not input-priced. Fund deliverables, not headcount.
Milestone-gated disbursement preferred. Lump-sum is a risk signal.
Open-source and public goods favored. Compounding ecosystem value over private extraction.
Calendar urgency is a manipulation signal. The rolling window has no boundary.
